title: tmuxcc session-proxy → client messages
description: All control-plane messages pushed by the session-proxy to a connected client. The session-proxy is the source of truth and pushes state changes; these are read-only events from the client's perspective.

SessionProxyCapabilitiesMessage.description: Session-proxy capabilities advertisement, sent once at handshake time (step 1 of the server-initiates handshake). direction: session-proxy→client.

SnapshotSession.description: The bound session as represented in a snapshot. Only carries identity — the session is always the bound session (always active) on the session-proxy wire.

SnapshotWindow.description: A window as represented in a snapshot.

active.description: True if this is the active window in the session.

synchronizePanes.description: True when synchronize-panes is on for this window at snapshot time. Added tc-7xv.12.

monitorActivity.description: True when monitor-activity is on for this window at snapshot time. Added tc-7xv.15.

monitorSilence.description: Current monitor-silence threshold in seconds, or 0 when disabled. Added tc-7xv.15.

SnapshotPane.description: A pane as represented in a snapshot.

SnapshotMessage.description: Full-state snapshot, sent once by the session-proxy immediately after the capabilities handshake. Normalized flat arrays; clients build their own in-memory tree by joining on ids. The session-proxy is bound to exactly one session (v3: single-session). direction: session-proxy→client.

windows.description: All windows in the bound session.

focus.description: Currently focused pane/window pair. Both are null if no pane is focused.

attachedClientCount.description: Number of session-proxy-protocol clients connected at snapshot time. Additive optional field (tc-1elae Phase 2). Live updates arrive via ClientCountChangedMessage (tc-44wu0).

PaneOpenedMessage.description: A new pane has been created. direction: session-proxy→client.

active.description: True if this pane is the active (focused) pane at the moment of opening.

PaneClosedMessage.description: A pane has been closed (exited or killed). direction: session-proxy→client.

exitCode.description: Exit code of the pane's process, if known. Absent when the session-proxy could not determine the exit code (non-breaking optional field).

PaneResizedMessage.description: A pane's dimensions have changed. Distinct from ResizeRequestMessage (client→session-proxy). direction: session-proxy→client.

PaneModeChangedMessage.description: A pane entered or left a mode (e.g. copy mode, normal mode). Open-ended mode string for forward compatibility. direction: session-proxy→client.

mode.description: Model-level pane mode. Known values: "normal", "copy", "view". Unknown future modes must be treated as opaque strings.

LayoutUpdatedMessage.description: The layout of a window has changed. Clients should apply atomically. direction: session-proxy→client.

FocusChangedMessage.description: The active (focused) pane has changed. If no pane is active, both paneId and windowId are null. direction: session-proxy→client.

WindowAddedMessage.description: A new window was added to the bound session. direction: session-proxy→client.

active.description: True if the new window immediately became the active window.

WindowClosedMessage.description: A window was closed. direction: session-proxy→client.

WindowRenamedMessage.description: A window was renamed. direction: session-proxy→client.

WindowSyncChangedMessage.description: The synchronize-panes state of a window has changed. Added tc-7xv.12. direction: session-proxy→client.

on.description: True when synchronize-panes is on for this window.

WindowMonitorActivityChangedMessage.description: The monitor-activity state of a window has changed. Added tc-7xv.15. direction: session-proxy→client.

on.description: True when monitor-activity is on for this window.

WindowMonitorSilenceChangedMessage.description: The monitor-silence state of a window has changed. Added tc-7xv.15. direction: session-proxy→client.

seconds.description: Silence threshold in seconds (positive = on), or 0 when disabled.

SessionProxySessionRenamedMessage.description: The bound session was renamed. No sessionId field — the bound session is implicit on the session-proxy wire (v3, tc-j9c). direction: session-proxy→client.

ClientCountChangedMessage.description: The number of session-proxy-protocol clients connected to this session has changed. Added tc-44wu0. direction: session-proxy→client.

count.description: Authoritative connected-client count at the moment the event is sent. On connect: includes the newly-connected client (≥1). On disconnect: excludes the just-disconnected client (≥0).

SessionProxyCommandOkPayload.description: Successful command result payload. Fields are optional because not every command produces a new entity. tc-ozk.1: for the pane/window-CREATING verbs (split-pane, open-window, break-pane) the session-proxy RETURNS the created entity's ids here — `paneId` is the created pane and `windowId` the window it lives in (recovered from tmux's `split-window`/`new-window`/`break-pane -P -F '#{pane_id} #{window_id}'` reply). The host binds by these returned ids when the pane materialises; no observer/claim correlation is needed.

info.description: session-proxy.info diagnostics payload (tc-x6l). Present only in session-proxy.info responses.

metricsText.description: Prometheus text exposition of all per-session-proxy counters and histograms.

stormWindowTotal.description: Total topology events in the current sliding window.

stormWindowBreakdown.description: Per-kind breakdown of events in the current sliding window.

SessionProxyCommandResponseMessage.description: The session-proxy's response to a SessionProxyCommandRequestMessage. Command-specific failures arrive here as result.ok=false; the separate ErrorMessage is for unsolicited/protocol-level errors. direction: session-proxy→client.

code.description: Error code. See WireErrorCode for known values.

ErrorMessage.description: Unsolicited error pushed by the session-proxy. ONLY for errors not attributable to a specific outstanding command request. For command-specific errors, the session-proxy sends a SessionProxyCommandResponseMessage with result.ok=false. direction: session-proxy→client.

code.description: Error code. Known values: "protocol.unknown-message", "protocol.malformed", "protocol.version-mismatch", "session.unavailable", "internal". Open-ended for forward compatibility.

correlationId.description: If set, ties this error to a prior SessionProxyCommandRequestMessage. Absent for fully unsolicited errors.

SessionProxyMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the session-proxy pushes to the client. Narrow on `type`.
